task_completion.description: Create the new customer_tier_history table in the EER model
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a new table named 'customer_tier_history' appearing in the MySQL Workbench EER diagram canvas or the schema tree.

task_completion.description: Add the required columns with correct data types and constraints
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the table editor pane showing the columns: 'history_id' (INT, PK, NN, AI checked), 'customer_id' (SMALLINT, NN checked), 'old_tier' (VARCHAR), 'new_tier' (VARCHAR), and 'change_date' (DATETIME).

task_completion.description: Establish a 1:n non-identifying relationship from customer to customer_tier_history
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a dashed relationship line connecting the 'customer' table to the 'customer_tier_history' table in the EER diagram, or the Foreign Keys tab in the table editor showing 'customer_id' referencing the 'customer' table.

task_completion.description: Save the EER model to the specified file path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save Model As' dialog targeting '/home/ga/Documents/sakila_loyalty_model.mwb' or the Workbench application tab/window title displaying 'sakila_loyalty_model.mwb'.

task_completion.description: Apply the model changes to the live sakila database
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Forward Engineer to Database' or 'Synchronize Model' wizard in MySQL Workbench, specifically showing the execution of the SQL script and a success message indicating the live database was updated.

integrity.description: Agent used MySQL Workbench visual modeling tools
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent interacted with the EER Diagram canvas and table editor GUI in MySQL Workbench, rather than just typing a CREATE TABLE SQL script in a terminal or basic text editor.

integrity.description: Agent used the built-in forward engineering/synchronization workflow
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the database update was performed through the MySQL Workbench 'Database -> Forward Engineer' or 'Database -> Synchronize Model' menu options, rather than manually copying and pasting SQL into a separate client.

integrity.description: Agent connected to the correct live database
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for connection details in the Forward Engineer/Synchronize wizard showing host 'localhost' and user 'ga'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected table name is 'customer_tier_history'. The expected columns are 'history_id' (INT, PK, AI, NN), 'customer_id' (SMALLINT(5), NN), 'old_tier' (VARCHAR(20)), 'new_tier' (VARCHAR(20)), and 'change_date' (DATETIME). The table must have a 1:n non-identifying foreign key relationship referencing the 'customer' table via the 'customer_id' column. The model must be saved exactly as '/home/ga/Documents/sakila_loyalty_model.mwb'.
